mission: Prove that standard hierarchical type-I seesaw leptogenesis still leaves a positive-dimensional source manifold after the closed late-time IO eta target is imposed.

assumptions: Casas-Ibarra parameterization for the source Yukawa sector

first_row_formulas.epsilon1: -(3 M1 / 16 pi v^2) * Im(sum_i m_i^2 R_1i^2) / (sum_i m_i |R_1i|^2)

theorem_1_dimension_count.statement: After fixing low-energy neutrino masses and a heavy-neutrino scale M1, the first row of the Casas-Ibarra matrix still carries four real degrees of freedom. Imposing one exact CP-asymmetry target removes only one real degree of freedom, leaving a positive-dimensional source manifold.

theorem_2_uniqueness_nogo.statement: The closed late-time IO eta target, even when combined with standard hierarchical type-I seesaw formulas, does not select a unique microscopic source point. Any uniqueness claim requires extra microphysical structure beyond the target product itself.
remaining_external_debt: an exact law selecting a point on the Casas-Ibarra source manifold
or an exact washout theorem fixing kappa_f from source data
or an exact UV completion collapsing the first-row manifold

theorem_3_explicit_continuum_witness.statement: At fixed M1 and fixed kappa_f, multiple distinct complex-angle source points reproduce the same exact target epsilon1. This explicitly verifies the positive-dimensional no-go.
